description_long: This bungalow is a single-story residential building with a sprawling, horizontally oriented layout and a prominent central entrance. The roof is a complex hip style, featuring multiple intersecting sections that add architectural interest and provide coverage for the extended wings. The exterior walls are clad in a light-colored material, likely siding or stucco, with contrasting trim around the windows and doors. The front facade includes a covered porch area supported by columns, creating a welcoming entryway. Windows are symmetrically arranged along the front and sides, with larger groupings near the central entrance and smaller sets on the wings. The structure sits on a flat, paved base, suggesting it is designed for suburban or urban neighborhood settings.
description: A wide, single-story bungalow with a complex hip roof, central covered porch, and symmetrically arranged windows on a light exterior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central covered porch, main entrance, and symmetrical window arrangement on either side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the central section and the hip roof's intersecting planes.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the extended wings and additional windows, with less architectural detailing than the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the rear wing and the continuation of the hip roof structure.
